Objective: Validate positive-edge triggered GPIO group interrupt across GPIO[8..39] with group enable and per-pin raw clear, iterating all pads.
TestSteps: The test begins by enabling the platform interrupt line for the selected GPIO instance via the interrupt controller and enabling the corresponding system-register interrupt. It then configures each GPIO pad from 8 through 39 to enable positive-edge detection by writing the control value with the positive-edge bit set. After a brief wait, it programs all four group IO control registers to set the GPIOs into input mode required for interrupt detection and waits again to allow the settings to take effect. It enables all per-group interrupt mask bits. For each pad index from 0 to 31, it first drives the shared pad-control lines low to ensure a known initial level and waits briefly. It sets a software pending flag to indicate it is awaiting an interrupt, then drives the shared pad-control lines high to create a single rising edge. It waits in a loop, with a finite timeout and short periodic delays, until the interrupt service routine clears the pending flag; if the timeout occurs, it records an error and breaks out. After servicing, it drives the shared pad-control lines low again and waits briefly before moving to the next pad. When all pads have been processed or a timeout has occurred, it finishes the test with the accumulated error count.
ExpectedResults: A rising edge on each pad triggers the ISR once, the group interrupt status becomes nonzero during handling, and the ISR clears the per-pin raw status, after which the group status reads as zero. No timeouts occur in the wait loops.
PassCriteria: The test passes if each induced rising edge leads to ISR execution that clears the software pending flag and the group interrupt status returns to zero after per-pin clears for every pad without any timeouts.
